name: Filename collision resolution strategy
description: Policy for choosing between colliding filenames when multiple sources or entities claim the same base name. Covers tie-breaking, auto-resolve vs escalate, and rename documentation.

level_1_primary_entity.rule: PRIMARY NAME of the parent entity wins. Entity with higher evidence_grade takes precedence.
level_1_primary_entity.logic: Compare frontmatter `sources: []` → resolve each source to entity → find entity with highest evidence_grade (see rules/evidence_grade.json). That entity's primary name becomes the prefix.

example.result: New ingest wins → rename existing to pi-cache.md, keep pi-cache.md as primary

level_2_specificity.rule: Most specific prefix wins over generic prefix.
level_2_specificity.logic: Count prefix depth: 'symfony-messenger' (depth=2) > 'symfony' (depth=1). Longer compound prefix = more specific = wins.

example.result: Existing bare page → rename to symfony-messenger.md (same prefix already captured)

level_3_last_updated.rule: If same specificity level and same entity, most recently updated page keeps its name; older page gets renamed.
level_3_last_updated.logic: Compare `date:` frontmatter field. Older → rename-page.sh to <prefix>-<name>.md

example.result: Older renamed to: symfony-cache.md, newer keeps cache.md

auto_resolve.action: RENAME bare → prefixed. Example: cache.md + symfony-cache.md (both about Symfony) → rename cache.md to symfony-cache-v1.md (alias 'cache.md'), merge content into symfony-cache.md

example.result: Rename cache.md → symfony-cache.md, add aliases: [cache], update related links

auto_resolve.action: RENAME older → prefixed. Apply level_1_primary_entity tie-breaking.

example.result: If both same entity (symfony), merge into one: symfony-cache.md or symfony-cache-system.md (most specific)

auto_resolve.action: ALLOW both. No collision. Example: symfony-cache.md + pi-cache.md are valid separate pages.

example.result: Both OK — add crosslink in related: [symfony-cache.md]

auto_resolve.action: ALLOW bare. Only enforce prefix if parent entity has primary name AND page content is entity-specific.

example.result: Bare cache.md is abstract concept — OK. symfony-cache.md is specific — OK. Crosslink both.

escalate_to_user.scenario: two prefixed names, SAME entity, DIFFERENT scope
escalate_to_user.action: ESCALATE. Example: symfony-messenger.md vs symfony-messaging.md — are they same concept or different? User decides.
escalate_to_user.note: Only escalate when specificity differs but entity is same AND scope ambiguity exists

escalate_to_user.action: ESCALATE. Cannot determine parent entity from sources/frontmatter tags.
escalate_to_user.note: Check rules/tag-guidelines.json#parent_entity_identification first — if still unclear, escalate

when_to_rename: Collision detected during process-ing ingest (collision_resolution step)
Two bare pages for same entity with different names (merge candidate)
how_to_rename: Update all related: links in other pages pointing to old name

rename_log_example.format:
## Renamed [YYYY-MM-DD]
Renamed from `<old_name>.md` to `<new_name>.md` — collision resolution: `<reason>` (tie-breaking: level N)
rename_log_example.example:
## Renamed 2026-07-19
Renamed from `cache.md` to `symfony-cache.md` — collision resolution: symfony primary entity wins over bare name (tie-breaking: level_1_primary_entity)

process_lint.output: JSON array of collision violations [{old_file, new_file, tie_breaking_level, resolved: true/false}]
process_lint.auto_fix: For resolved=true: apply rename. For resolved=false: add to next_steps_todo in working_memory.json

examples.action: Rename cache.md → symfony-cache.md, add aliases: [cache], update wiki/concepts/cache.md → symfony-cache.md in all related links

examples.case: same prefix, same entity, different specificity
examples.action: Merge if content overlaps significantly (>50%). Rename older → keep newer prefix
